intra_AIS.note: `return -1` is control-dependent on the guard's true arm
`const y = x * 2` runs only when the guard is false: control-dependent
`const z = y + 1` is control-dependent on the guard (and data-dependent on y)
`return z` is control-dependent on the guard

rationale: Criterion is the guard predicate `if (!ok)` (line 7). Its CDG controller->dependent edges reach the true-arm `return -1` (line 9) and the post-guard body (lines 11-13), which execute only on the complementary arm. All dependents are statements WITHIN `guarded`; the function calls nothing. intra_AIS captures every control-dependent line; inter_AIS empty. This is the canonical guard-clause CDG shape (#559) and isolates the CDG-forward arm of KTD4 — PDG distinguishes control-affected statements that the call-graph mode cannot resolve below function granularity.
